author: 305 Trader
content: HI. I reviewed this set up just now. This set up served very well the 1st 15 min candle ORB strategy but to the d/s.  It did retest even twice the low. Now, only thing I can say against that CALL was that on the Daily chart the stock was overextended in relationship to the ADR of the previous 9 days. Context is so important. The stock opened up following a huuge gap, so I would have wanted to wait and observe what direction it would pick. There is this rule that I try reminding myself that says: Do not buy stock that is overextended to either side.  Oscar, buy CALLS in discount area and go short in Premium area. This simple yet tried and true notion can save you from FOMO and from doing a bad entry. Everything is possible, yes, but the odds are not in our favor when we buy when the stock or index is overextended.

author: Kuba
content:
Hi

I wanted to share 3 trades I made yestarday, allowing me to make a relatively small profit as I only trade with 1 contracts. 

#1 HOOD 5m low retest with a low risk just above the candle which broke the ORB. TP was a psychological number of 72.00. Unfortunately at 10:34 it hit 72.01 (!) and went back up so I chickened out and took some profits but this trade would have worked out nicely if I had the balls to hold it. Need to work on this part. This would have been a nice 1:3.14 RR and even more as it went further down to $71.50-ish and $71-ish later on.

#2 TSLA premarket high retest with low risk just below the PMH level and TP at 320.00 round level. There was a nice volume spike on the breakout candle and a quick reaction on the retest so I decided to enter at 11:10 candle. Of course I chickened out and didn't stay till it hit my target but got an easy 1:2 RR. This was a 1:5 RR opportunity for me.

#3 GOOGL a couple of retests really: 1m opening candle, 5m range and a pre-market high. This one is the strangest trades I've ever came across, it suddenly spiked up due to some news I believe, however my risk was also very low here with 1 con, just below the 9:43 candle and I was simply aiming for a 1:2 RR. I took profits somewhere around $178.30 level as, again, I chickened out with my 1 con to run. 


I believe I had decent setups, but I just keep screwing up my exits due to fear of loosing profits. Any feedback would be appreciated.

author: Kam
content:
Took a pretty lousy trade on PLTR this morning and wanted to share my review. Open to any thoughts or other feedback! 

PLTR – 6/26/25
Setup: BnR 1m OB Continuation + 5m OR Retest
Result:  Loss (–1R)
Confidence on Entry: 3/5
Sizing: Base Size

What Happened:
Strong opening drive in PLTR with RS and news catalyst. Took an anticipatory reclaim entry off the 1m OB after visual strength near prior pivot, but skipped confirmation and entered on candle stretch. Market context was improving, 5m candle looked like a nice hammer, but pullback was steep and PLTR can be trappy. Trade hit stop shortly after entry. Earlier missed trade off PMH may have been a factor as well as pass on NVDA OB play prior day.

Classification:
False Form – Anticipatory Entry 
Good structure idea, poor entry execution. Trade plan shifted from OB pullback to reclaim without re-evaluation.

Primary Mistake:
Premature Entry – No Reclaim Confirmation

What Worked:
- Avoided forced dip buys in semis
- Honored stop and respected system
- Solid structure read on RS and catalyst

Needs Work:
– Entry discipline on reclaims
– Slight emotional residue from passing on NVDA prior day and PMH retest at the open
– OB sequence clarity and reclaim filters need reinforcement

author: Jatin
content: Hi, I am looking to get some feedback on the trade I took this morning. Entered in at 9:57 candle with confluences with QQQ and SMH with 2 cons. 1st profit target at HOD and was then following strictly order blocks for continuation with the last con but stopped out at 10:07 candle. I did not wait for the candle closure below $155.60 but exited on the wick as I wanted to avoid any losses that was a mistake I realize. Anything else that I could have done differently. Any feedback is much appreciated.

author: Kam
content:
Hey Jatin - this is a tricky one for me since it's lower timeframe bearish, but HTF bullish. I didn't take this trade, but here's how I hypothetically would have managed it:

The 155 area was the reclaim level after the 5m hammer into the PDH level so once we get a candle close above that level I'd draw out the full OB and use that as structural invalidation. I'd have taken profit like you at HOD. That nasty wick held that OB - and after taking profits at HOD, the trade is de-risked so if it comes back it comes back, and stop out for BE or small loss depending on your size. I probably would have sold into new high pops at 156.5, but these setups usually work fast and hit PMH quickly. So once it stalled out, I probably would have been cautious when got back under the HOD pivot and 1m/5m OB to stop out of trailers. 

There's different ways to trail though - I tend to use OB's and structure to trail, but others like <@1121621751764369501> use trendlines and EMAs. <@1014389514141184074> uses a 5m timer! Just have to find your style!

Easy to play armchair QB with hindsight bias i know 😂  But that's my 2c, thanks for asking - this was a good thought exercise!

I know Tony & Jack took this trade cos they were just talking about it in Traders Lab - so definitely check out Tony's trade review of this tonight!

author: 305 Trader
content:
hi Francine. I thought of making a review to your trade, because I found it pretty good a teaching for me. You got the direction right, as you can see Apple had been on a upward ride for days. So, yes momentum had been there, thats 1. Now as for your entry, since AAPL demonstrated that the uptrend was clear for several days, that bounce from your wedge in purple lines was your POI and not the ORB you drew. Additionally, the break out from that wedge had confluence with one of your KL. This was a powerful event you should have not missed.  If I was to take a swing or even a day trade on AAPL, it would have been on the break and retest (in a smaller tf) of that confluence. In the higher timeframe (swing) this was also, the continuation signal of the market in its uptrend. So, your entry was late when you decided to make it off of that ORB way above. 
Or, if taking the swing off of that ORB, it would need to be after the mitigation with a strong rejection to that POI or ORB you drew. I think you took it on the 1st impulse instead  and hence you had the expected pullback. I am curious how the symbol did on Friday since I had not traded since last Tuesday.

author: Ruslan /ruˈslan/
content:
Guys, I want to share my crappy day because of my own stupidity. The story is actually kind of funny 🙂
I took 1 TSLA 302.5 Put 0DTE contract, held it the whole time while the price was falling, and as soon as it reversed after 298, I couldn’t sell—like hypnotized—waiting for it to go back down, all the way until it climbed up to 301.5, and only then did I lock in a $30 profit, even though I could’ve made a few hundred bucks.
And then classic: I immediately jumped into a revenge trade and locked in a $70 loss.
I thought I’d learned to get out on a mental stop-loss (sometimes it’s tough mentally, especially after red days), but I didn’t expect myself to hesitate and not take the profit.
Just wanted to share the reflection 🙂
To everyone who’s green today—respect. And to those who aren’t—also, because we’re still here and still trying to get better.

author: EeveeWinter
content: You were in a 332.5C, with the underlying moving to 335.10. But for most of the trade: You were initially OTM — The move took time — Delta was likely sub-0.50 for a good chunk of the time. Since delta starts low and only ramps up as price nears the strike, your option didn’t gain value linearly. Early in the move, delta was low; as the move slowed, delta rose — but then theta canceled the gain.

author: FierceTiger
content: For reference, check out yesterday's charts on AMD, TSLA and GOOG. Any long taken at any point after PM low has been taken out would have resulted in profit. My advice is to always zoom out, have a HTF in mind and have the conviction to hold trades until the price target is met (which in today's case is at the end of the day). Of course, as what is taught here, you need to de-risk 80% of your position to ensure BE at the first big candle pop or 2R. Only trailers should be held until EOD or overnight.
